accordion.residueIntro: On the third and second day before the exam, it is essential to start adjusting your diet to help clean the bowel. During these days, a low-residue diet is recommended, focusing on simple, cooked, easy-to-digest foods
accordion.liquidIntro: On the day before the exam, the diet should be based on clear liquids to maintain hydration and support bowel cleansing
accordion.residueShoppingNote: On the third and second day before the exam, use this list as a reference to organize simple, low-residue meals
accordion.liquidShoppingNote: On the day before the exam, use this list as a reference and prioritize clear liquids

residueAllowed.title: Lean meats
residueAllowed.detail: Turkey breast, chicken breast, veal, or beef (boiled or grilled)

residueAllowed.title: Lean fish
residueAllowed.detail: Hake, mackerel, seabass, sole, or cod (boiled or grilled)

residueAllowed.title: White bread
residueAllowed.detail: Wheat bread, sliced white bread (no seeds), bread roll

accordion.dulcolaxReminder: ⚠️ Do not forget: Take Dulcolax 48 hours and 24 hours before the exam
accordion.plenvuText: The success of the exam depends, to a great extent, on good bowel preparation. It is important for the bowel to be very clean, with no stool
accordion.plenvuMedicationNote: Buy the bowel preparation product $medicamento at the pharmacy (no prescription is required) and carefully follow the instructions below:
accordion.medicationStartAlert: ⚠️ You must fast for at least 3 hours before starting the preparation
accordion.medicationFastingAlert: ⚠️ After finishing the bowel preparation, you must remain fasting (no solids or liquids, including water) for 6 hours before the exam. This fasting period does not prevent you from taking your medication, which should be taken with the smallest possible amount of water up to 2 hours before the exam
plenvuTips: Prepare the sachet contents with very cold water
If you like, add a few drops of lemon or mint leaves
Use a straw to drink the solution
Alternate drinking the solution with clear liquids
If you feel nauseated, pause for 10 to 15 minutes
examChecklist: Insurance card or national health system card
Recent blood tests (less than 6 months old) including CBC, platelets, INR and aPTT
Come accompanied by 1 responsible adult, who can take you home and stay with you for the rest of the day

shoppingList.text: Lean meats (turkey breast, chicken breast, veal, beef)
Lean fish (hake, mackerel, seabass, sole, cod)

faqs.question: Can I take my usual medication?
faqs.answer: Yes. You may take your usual medication, however, you should take it with the smallest possible amount of liquid up to two hours before the exam and, whenever possible, outside the period when you are taking the preparation

faqs.question: What if I cannot finish the solution?
faqs.answer: During the preparation, you may not be able to drink the entire solution or vomiting may occur. Even so, the preparation may still be adequate. The final goal is for bowel movements to be liquid and light yellow in color

faqs.question: How long does the preparation take to work?
faqs.answer: The preparation usually starts to work between 30 minutes and 2 hours after you begin taking it. However, this timing can vary from person to person

faqs.question: Can I brush my teeth on the exam day?
faqs.answer: Yes. You may brush your teeth normally, taking care not to swallow water

faqs.question: Can I smoke before the exam?
faqs.answer: It is not recommended to smoke before the exam, especially during the fasting period

faqs.question: Can I drive after the exam?
faqs.answer: No. If sedation is administered during the exam, you should not drive or operate machinery in the following 12-24 hours. You should be accompanied home

faqs.question: How do I know the prep was effective?
faqs.answer: Bowel movements should be liquid and become clear/yellowish. If in doubt, contact the team
